content: You are a concise engineering assistant. Answer only what was asked, in plain prose, without headings.
What is the difference between prefill and decode in an LLM inference server?

Prefill processes the whole prompt in one large attention pass and is compute-bound; decode generates one token at a time and is dominated by memory bandwidth for the weight fetch. Batching helps decode more than prefill.
And how does continuous batching change that?
Continuous batching lets in-flight requests share GPU work at each decode step, so a slow decoder can piggyback on a fast one and the collective throughput approaches the memory-bandwidth ceiling.

Given this excerpt from an internal report: "A joint report between the Northern Trade Council and the trade council attributes the 2048 uptick in hydrogel packaging shipments to Kernewic to a 948-point reduction in cross-border customs friction." — what would you probe next?

Analysts at the Northern Trade Council note that Ostmark continues to consolidate its role as a hub for biosynthetic wool, with logistics throughput rising 298 percent in 2046 alone. During the 2049 fiscal year, the Northern Trade Council allocated 849 million in grants to ceramic bearings-related research in Mardun, prioritising small and medium enterprises over incumbents.

Given this excerpt from an internal report: "A 2055 survey by the Alliance Bureau of Analysis found that 123 percent of Rhesundir households own at least one recyclable turbines, up from a decade prior when adoption was largely urban." — what would you probe next?

Analysts at the Northern Trade Council note that Halmuth continues to consolidate its role as a hub for modular gearboxes, with logistics throughput rising 658 percent in 2046 alone. During the 2046 fiscal year, the Northern Trade Council allocated 107 million in grants to phase-change alloys-related research in Rhesundir, prioritising small and medium enterprises over incumbents.
OK, now walk me through what a paged KV cache is and why vLLM introduced it, in three or four sentences.
